mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and income growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward 20-day SPX returns is negative over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the tightening cycle becomes restrictive enough to compress economic activity and raise equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS breaches 4.5%, forward 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based demand momentum; when both exceed their 12-month moving average, the economy is expanding robustly, supporting positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances in walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by a 1% rise in the federal funds rate over six months, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals overheating and the likelihood of further tightening, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increased by 1% over the past 6 months
failure_condition: During such tightening cycles, INDPRO growth has a positive correlation with forward returns for more than 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals consumer health; above-trend growth supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS year-over-year growth is negative and forward 60-day SPX returns remain positive in more than 40% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, the labor market becomes extremely tight, fueling wage inflation and anticipated Fed tightening, which raises equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, the average forward 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed is raising rates (FEDFUNDS increasing month over month) at the same time as employment is growing strongly (PAYEMS above its 12-month moving average), the economy risks overheating, forcing aggressive tightening that depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase > 0 and PAYEMS > 12-month MA
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a non-negative average forward 20-day return over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal a pending contraction in manufacturing activity and overall economic weakness, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: After two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, the average 5-day forward SPX return is not negative or is positive in more than 50% of occurrences.
